---
title: "LLM-assisted topic modelling of Canadian COVID-19 Immunity Task Force data sharing operations"
date: today
bibliography: references.bib
authors:
  - name: Zachary Batist
    id: zb
    email: zachary.batist@mcgill.ca
    url: https://zackbatist.info
    orcid: 0000-0003-0435-508X
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Epidemiology, Biostatistics and Occupational Health
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
  - name: Melissa Ouellet
    id: mo
    email: melissa.ouellet@mcgill.ca
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Epidemiology, Biostatistics and Occupational Health
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
  - name: Sadun Khezri
    id: sk
    email: sadun.khezri@mcgill.ca
    orcid: 0000-0001-6235-6668
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Epidemiology, Biostatistics and Occupational Health
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
  - name: Tanya Murphy
    id: tm
    email: tanya.murphy@mcgill.ca
    orcid: 0000-0002-9643-8730
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Epidemiology, Biostatistics and Occupational Health
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
  - name: Aklil Noza
    id: an
    email: aklil.noza@mcgill.ca
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Epidemiology, Biostatistics and Occupational Health
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
  - name: Samira Abbasgholizadeh-Rahimi
    id: sar
    email: samira.rahimi@mcgill.ca
    orcid: 0000-0003-3781-1360
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Department of Family Medicine
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
  - name: Guillaume Bourque
    id: gb
    email: guil.bourque@mcgill.ca
    orcid: 0000-0002-3933-9656
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Department of Human Genetics
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
  - name: David Buckeridge
    id: db
    email: david.buckeridge@mcgill.ca
    orcid: 0000-0003-1817-5047
    affiliations:
      - name: McGill University
        department: Epidemiology, Biostatistics and Occupational Health
        city: Montreal
        region: Québec
        country: Canada
abstract: |
  **Background:** xxx.\
  **Methods:** xxx.\
  **Results:** xxx.\
  **Conclusions:** xxx.\
  
  This study analyzes 6,833 emails from the inbox of a central operations manager at the Canadian COVID-19 Immunity Task Force (CITF) Databank, spanning correspondence with 100 partnering studies from February 2021 to September 2025, to characterize the operational processes of large-scale health research data sharing. Emails were preprocessed using rule-based cleaning and automated de-identification, with French text translated into English using neural machine translation. Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) was applied for topic modeling, selecting k=14 topics based on coherence, granularity, and topic diversity. Topic labels and thematic groupings were generated using a locally deployed large language model (Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct) and refined by the research team. Topics were analyzed across six phases of the data sharing lifecycle and five sender categories. NMF identified 14 topics organized into four thematic categories: Agreements & Contracts, Data Operations, Study Documentation, and Coordination. Governance and data operations workstreams ran largely in parallel, with contractual and administrative concerns persisting throughout all lifecycle phases rather than being resolved early. Coordination labour was highly concentrated among Databank personnel (up to 116 emails per sender) relative to study teams (5--7 emails per sender), with women more concentrated in Study Documentation topics and men in Data Operations topics. The 18:1 ratio of administrative to technical time reveals that data sharing is primarily an administrative and governance challenge, with direct implications for how Canadian research data infrastructure is resourced, governed, and staffed under national policy frameworks.

keywords:
  - topic modeling
  - research data sharing
  - data governance
  - COVID-19
  - Canada
filters: 
  - ../assets/count-para.lua
format:
  html:
    number-sections: false
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - top = 1in
      - bottom = 1in
      - left = 1in
      - right = 1in
    colorlinks: true
    toc: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{orcidlink}
        \usepackage{marvosym}
    template-partials:
      - ../assets/title.tex
---

<!-- ```{r setup}

```

```{r dependencies}

```

```{r plot-theme}

``` -->

{{< include sections/01_background.qmd >}}
{{< include sections/02_methods.qmd >}}
{{< include sections/03_results.qmd >}}
{{< include sections/04_discussion.qmd >}}
{{< include sections/05_conclusions.qmd >}}
{{< include sections/10_references.qmd >}}
{{< include sections/11_back-matter.qmd >}}
<!-- {{< include sections/12_supplement.qmd >}} -->
